reasoning: The European Central Bank recently paused its rate-cutting cycle in July 2025, holding the deposit facility rate at 2.0%. Market consensus over the upcoming year strongly favors either unchanged rates or a further 25 basis point cut by the end of 2025 (money markets price in 14 basis points of easing by year-end). While a few contrarian analysts and firms, such as Deutsche Bank and BNP Paribas, recently modified their forecasts to predict the *next* move to be a rate hike, both predict this potential hike would not occur until late 2026 (e.g., Q4 2026 or after a US-EU tariff deal). Therefore, the probability of an actual rate increase in 2025 or early 2026 is extremely low. I estimate 5% for August 2025 due to zero scheduled meetings, slowly expanding to 14% by January 2026 to account for inflation tail-risks and general forecasting uncertainty at longer horizons.
